title: Boundary Setting Worksheet
description: Use this worksheet to plan, practice, and reflect on setting a boundary. Work through each step to prepare before the conversation, and return afterward to record what happened.
instructions: Identify a situation where you need to set a boundary. Work through each step to clarify what you want, prepare your script, anticipate fears, and reflect on the outcome. This worksheet is for planning — you can complete the follow-up section after you've tried setting the boundary.

fields.label: 1. Situation — What boundary do you need to set? With whom?
fields.placeholder: e.g., My manager keeps sending messages after 7 PM expecting immediate responses...

fields.label: 2. Current Pattern — What do you usually do in this situation? How does it affect you?
fields.placeholder: e.g., I respond immediately even though I'm off the clock. It means I never fully disconnect from work, and I feel resentful and exhausted.

fields.label: 3. Stress Level — How much does this situation stress you? (0 = not at all, 100 = extremely)
4. Boundary Fear — What are you afraid will happen if you set this boundary?

options.label: Rejection — They'll think less of me or distance themselves
Perceived Laziness — They'll think I don't care or I'm not committed
Conflict — It will create an argument or tension
Consequences — I'll be passed over, punished, or fired
Guilt — I'll feel selfish or like I'm letting people down

fields.label: 5. Boundary Script — What will you actually say? (Be specific and clear)
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'I'm going to be offline after 7 PM on weekdays. I'll respond to any messages first thing the next morning. For true emergencies, you can call me.'

fields.label: 6. Fear Response — What is a more balanced view of your fear? What's the most likely outcome?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Most likely, my manager will understand — other team members have similar boundaries. Even if there's initial pushback, my work quality will improve because I'll be less exhausted.

fields.label: 7. Follow-Up — What happened when you set the boundary? (Complete this after trying)
fields.placeholder: e.g., I told my manager on Monday. She seemed surprised but said 'that's fair.' No negative consequences so far. I felt guilty the first two evenings but it got easier.

fields.label: 8. Stress Level After — How stressed do you feel about this situation now? (0 = not at all, 100 = extremely)

distortionsList.name: Fear of Rejection
distortionsList.description: Believing that setting a boundary will make people dislike or distance themselves from you. In reality, most people respect clear, consistent limits.

distortionsList.name: Fear of Being Seen as Lazy
distortionsList.description: Assuming that having limits means you lack commitment. In reality, boundaries protect your ability to do sustainable, high-quality work.

distortionsList.name: Fear of Conflict
distortionsList.description: Expecting that any limit-setting will create a confrontation. In reality, clear, calm communication often prevents conflict rather than creating it.

distortionsList.name: Fear of Professional Consequences
distortionsList.description: Worrying about being punished, passed over, or fired for setting reasonable limits. While some environments are genuinely punitive, many are more accepting of boundaries than we expect.

distortionsList.name: Guilt and People-Pleasing
distortionsList.description: Feeling selfish for prioritizing your needs. In reality, you cannot pour from an empty cup — boundaries help you show up better for others, not worse.

distortionsList.name: Other Fears
distortionsList.description: Any other fear or concern about setting this boundary. Write it out and examine whether it's based on evidence or assumption.

disclaimer: This worksheet is for educational purposes to help you practice boundary-setting skills. If you're in a situation where setting boundaries puts your safety at risk (e.g., abusive relationships or hostile work environments), please seek professional guidance. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
